2606.22528.title: Governance Decay: How Context Compaction Silently Erases Safety Constraints in Long-Horizon LLM Agents
2606.22528.paper_summary: Modern LLM agents increasingly rely on context compaction, summarization, or eviction to keep long-running sessions within a token budget. We show that this context-management layer is a safety-critical failure surface: in-context governance constraints that agents reliably obey while visible can be silently removed by compaction, causing the same agent to perform prohibited tool actions later in the session. We call this failure mode Governance Decay. We introduce ConstraintRot, a benchmark of long-horizon agent scenarios with deterministic tool-call grading, and measure compaction-induced violations across seven model families. Across 1,323 episodes, violation rises from 0% with the policy in full context to 30% after compaction, reaching 59% for some models; when the constraint survives the summary, violation remains 0%, but when it is dropped, violation reaches 38%. We further study a Compaction-Eviction Attack, in which adversarial in-context content biases the summarizer to omit a legitimate policy, and show that optimized injections defeat every evaluated model. Finally, we propose Constraint Pinning, a simple training-free mitigation that quarantines governance constraints from lossy compaction and restores violation to 0% in our benchmark. These results identify context management as a first-class governance surface for deployed LLM agents.
2606.22528.paper_summary_zh: 現代的 LLM 代理越來越依賴上下文壓縮、摘要或驅逐來保持長時間運行的會話在標記預算內。<lb/>我們展示了這個上下文管理層是一個安全關鍵的失效面：在可見的情況下，代理可靠遵守的上下文治理約束可以被壓縮靜默移除，導致同一代理在會話後期執行禁止的工具行為。我們稱這種失效模式為治理衰退。<lb/>我們引入了 ConstraintRot，一個具有確定性工具調用評分的長期代理場景基準，並測量七個模型家族中因壓縮引起的違規情況。在 1,323 集中，違規從完整上下文中的 0% 上升到壓縮後的 30%，對於某些模型達到 59%；當約束在摘要中存活時，違規保持在 0%，但當它被移除時，違規達到 38%。<lb/>我們進一步研究了一種壓縮-驅逐攻擊，其中對抗性的上下文內容使摘要者偏向於省略合法政策，並顯示優化的注入擊敗了每個評估的模型。<lb/>最後，我們提出了約束固定，這是一種簡單的無需訓練的緩解方法，將治理約束與有損壓縮隔離，並在我們的基準中將違規恢復到 0%。<lb/>這些結果確定了上下文管理作為部署的 LLM 代理的一流治理表面。
2606.22528.author: Shiyang Chen et.al.
